title: Warm Argentinian Chicken Stew
description: A kid-friendly, creamy stew that blends spicy yellow curry paste with tempered chocolate for depth, topped with fresh cilantro and baby spinach. This paleo-friendly dish can be served as a comforting lunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat the avocado oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and yellow curry paste, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced chicken breast and cook until lightly browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and chicken broth, then add the sweet potato cubes and sliced carrot.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, reduce heat to low, and cook for 20 minutes until the vegetables soften.
Tempered cacao nibs can be added by stirring them into the stew; they melt and give a subtle chocolate depth.
Fold in the baby spinach and simmer until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Season with salt, pepper, and chopped cilantro, stirring gently.
Serve hot in bowls, spooning the creamy sauce over the chicken and veggies.
